messages.author: verdi
messages.text: https://meshcoresim.com You can upload the neighbour lists from your repeater via bt to your companion. If enough people do it you can simulate how messages would travel in your local mesh. With configurable hop limit and SNR filter.
messages.content_html: https://meshcoresim.com You can upload the neighbour lists from your repeater via bt to your companion. If enough people do it you can simulate how messages would travel in your local mesh. With configurable hop limit and SNR filter.
embeds_html:
MeshCore Mesh Simulator
Visualize MeshCore mesh network repeaters and their connections
messages.reactions_html:
1
2
2

messages.author: creeg2
messages.text: Does this app auto poll or is it all manual?
messages.content_html: Does this app auto poll or is it all manual?

messages.author: creeg2
messages.text: Do all repeaters accept neighbor requests without password, or do you have to be "allowed"?
messages.content_html: Do all repeaters accept neighbor requests without password, or do you have to be "allowed"?

messages.author: verdi
messages.text: you have to be allowed or guest password must be empty
messages.content_html: you have to be allowed or guest password must be empty
messages.reply_html:
creeg2
Do all repeaters accept neighbor requests without password, or do you have to be "allowed"?

messages.author: verdi
messages.text: what do you mean by that? After connecting it automatically syncs contacts, channels and messages and then you choose the repeater manually and click on "Get Neighbours". It then automatically retrieves the Nieghbour list and then you click on upload.
messages.content_html: what do you mean by that? After connecting it automatically syncs contacts, channels and messages and then you choose the repeater manually and click on "Get Neighbours". It then automatically retrieves the Nieghbour list and then you click on upload.
messages.reply_html:
creeg2
Does this app auto poll or is it all manual?

messages.author: creeg2
messages.text: I was asking if the app would automatically periodically go through the list of repeaters (from the connected client) and automatically poll and post the neighbors. This would allow users to monitor the status of a repeater-repeater connection over time. This would use a lot of airtime though. Maybe it could be scheduled to occur in the middle of the night. And/or you could toggle certain repeaters of interest to be on "auto" keeping the rest on manual refresh.
messages.content_html: I was asking if the app would automatically periodically go through the list of repeaters (from the connected client) and automatically poll and post the neighbors. This would allow users to monitor the status of a repeater-repeater connection over time. This would use a lot of airtime though. Maybe it could be scheduled to occur in the middle of the night. And/or you could toggle certain repeaters of interest to be on "auto" keeping the rest on manual refresh.
messages.reply_html:
verdi
what do you mean by that? After connecting it automatically syncs contacts, channels and messages and then you choose the repeater manually and click on "Get Neighbours". It then automatically retrieves the Nieghbour list and then you click on upload.

messages.author: verdi
messages.text: ah now i get it. thx for clarifying. at the moment it can't do it. and it only updates if the last heard timestamp changes. but i'm thinking about building maybe a desktop app that could do that.
messages.content_html: ah now i get it. thx for clarifying. at the moment it can't do it. and it only updates if the last heard timestamp changes. but i'm thinking about building maybe a desktop app that could do that.

messages.author: verdi
messages.text: Version 3.0.1 is out! It’s all about a smoother, clearer experience: Virtual (interpolated) repeaters make the map feel more complete Faster, more consistent loading as you explore Cleaner visuals and polished UI touches Better guidance in the FAQ when you need help Updates feel more immediate and reliable Thanks for all the feedback
messages.content_html:
Version 3.0.1 is out! It’s all about a smoother, clearer experience:
  • Virtual (interpolated) repeaters make the map feel more complete
  • Faster, more consistent loading as you explore
  • Cleaner visuals and polished UI touches
  • Better guidance in the FAQ when you need help
  • Updates feel more immediate and reliable
Thanks for all the feedback
